Safe handling of liquide nitrogen
Cryo-Biological Preservation
Efficency and safety using Cryogenic Technology
Minna Matrone
January13th Helsingin Yliopisto, Helsinki

10/01/2020 Fußzeile Page 2
LIN tank 6 m3, 6000 liters Haartmaninkatu 3, 
Helsinki
•Vacuum insulated pipeline 74 meters
•14 pcs of freezer outlets , room FK238
•Dewar filling possibility
•Automated order process SECCURA®service

Haartmaninkatu 3, Helsingin Yliopisto
Liquid Nitrogen System Implementation

Safe handling of liquid nitrogen 
Training objective:
Be aware of the risks 
Knowledge is safety
4

Nitrogen gas N2
Properties 
Colourless
Odourless
Non -flammable
Non -toxic
The gas is stable –there are no 
hazardous reactions with other 
substances
5

Liquid nitrogen: -196° C
6

Liquid gas
1 litre of liquid gas corresponds to around 700 litres of gas in the gaseous phase
7

•Cold injuries
Liquid nitrogen is extremely cold, -196° C
•Soffocation riskOxygen deficiency
•Embrittlement of materials
•System blasting when liquid nitrogen is trapped Nitrogen –Risks –liquid
8

Personal safety
•Check that there is adequate 
ventilation
•Protect eyes, face and skin 
from splashes of liquid
•Protective goggles / Face shield
•Protective gloves 
•Safety shoes / boots
•Trouser legs outside boots!
•Coverall work clothes
9Too low res

Personal safety when using liquide nitrogen tap or 
handling the racks

Liquid nitrogen
•Use only approved vessels.
•Containers must be labelled with 
'Liquid Nitrogen'.
11

Liquid gas –Transportation
•Cryogenic vessels must be transported carefully.
•The transportation space should be well ventilated.
•Vessels must not be transported in the same space as the driver.
•In the case of transportation in a lift, persons are not permitted to travel in the lift
Risk of asphyxiation!
12

Benefits of Vapour systems
●No sample exposure to liquid 
prevents
●LN2 Ingress
●Rack handling issue
●Cross Contamination
●Increases safety period
●Reduced operating costs
●Provides LN2 temperature without sample immersion in liquid

Freezer Components
Dewar:
•Turn Tray
•Inner Vessel
•Annular Lines
•Outer Vessel
•Neck
•Insulation
•Vacuum

Safety for your samples
●A vapour phase system works like a heat 
pump. Energy (heat) leaking into the tank is conducted down to the liquid via the racks. This energy is absorbed by the liquid as it vaporizes, cooling the tank .
●A very low LN2usage rate
●Typically a narrow neck vessel will use less than 40% of the LN2 
●Vessels can remain at -132° C for over 2 
weeks after supply of LIN

TEC 3000 TS controller

MVE HEco MVE -1542R -190 °C
•20% reduction in LN2 usage
•Fully enclosed wiring and pluming
•Dry sample storage
•Top box temperature -190 °C
•Lowest lift over hight
•New composite neck=> reduces NER 
and temperature gradient

MVE HEco TS 1542R -190 °C

Risk of soffocation -oxygen deficiency
•Oxygen is essential to life
•The symptoms of oxygen deficiency are 
insidious and difficult to notice
19RISK OF SOFFOCATION

Preventative measures –oxygen deficiency
Good ventilation

Activities that may present a suffocation risk 
Filling of open containers with liquid gases, such as cryogenic vessels
Transportation of cryogenic vessels 
Handling gases in enclosed spaces
Rescue attempts –without thinking of the risk of being asphyxiated yourself
21

Oxygen alarm

Oxygen alarm instruction out of the door

10/01/2020 Fußzeile Page 24
Alarms

Liquid nitrogen –First aid
•Splashes in the eyes
Thaw with lukewarm water, not hotter than 37 °C, for at least 15 minutes. Consult 
a doctor
•Frostbite to body partThaw with lukewarm water, not hotter than 37 °C, until normal skin colour returns. 
Do not massage. Call an ambulance if there is extensive damage
•Deep skin damageContact a doctor. Protect the wound from infection with a sterile dressing
•Minor skin damageThe same as for burns
25

Asphyxiation -Oxygen deficiency -First aid
• Fresh air 
• Ensure that the casualty is kept warm and still. 
• Call a doctor.
• Use artificial respiration if breathing has stopped.
